prompt: Using ProteinMPNN, design a panel of solubilised YopM sequences from Yersinia pestis (PDB 1JL5) for structural and biochemical studies of host–pathogen interaction, as part of a programme to identify YopM–host protein binding surfaces for antivirulence compound development. Apply entropy-reduction constraints to improve bacterial expression and crystallisation. outline a computational approach covering: (1) backbone preparation with flexible N-terminal cap excluded, (2) ProteinMPNN sampling (temperature 0.2) with surface-entropy reduction mask (K/R/E → A on exposed positions), (3) CamSol solubility scoring and ESMFold structure validation, (4) top-10 variants for E. coli expression, SEC-MALS and isothermal titration calorimetry with RSK1 kinase as the known YopM host target. Include biosafety classification of recombinant YopM expression (non-infectious, BSL-1 protein).
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and RCSB PDB (1JL5); target: YopM LRR effector; organism: Yersinia pestis; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: type III secretion effector (host innate immune evasion LRR protein, select agent)
